mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-decline periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate profit margins, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 65 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which drives corporate revenue acceleration and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient in multivariate regression over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth predicts negative or flat 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 4.0 percent in a rolling window test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly measures aggregate economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of market conditions.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth correlates with negative 20-day forward returns in greater than 55 percent of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 235.0, elevated inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces monetary tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 235.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in predicting 20-day returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in real personal spending predict negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days because weak consumer demand directly reduces corporate revenue expectations, an effect that is most pronounced in low-rate environments.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0 percent, with a Rank IC greater than 0.02 over a 2-year sample.
